title: Threads 3
description: Channels provide message passing between threads, following Rust's motto: 'Do not communicate by sharing memory; instead, share memory by communicating.'
instructions: Use channels to send messages between threads.
hints: Clone the transmitter for each thread: let tx = tx.clone();
Use tx.send(msg).unwrap() to send a message.
Drop the original tx so the channel closes when all threads finish.
explanation: Channels (`mpsc::channel`) implement message passing between threads. `mpsc` stands for Multiple Producer, Single Consumer. Each thread gets a cloned transmitter (`tx`) and sends messages to the single receiver (`rx`). Iterating over `rx` blocks waiting for messages and ends when all transmitters are dropped. You must drop the original `tx` so only the thread clones remain.
